sourceSummary.summary: The meeting focused on Pinnacle Logistics AP Automation: Exception handling and human review queue; Slack approval workflow for invoice approvals. Slack approval workflow increment: $3,000 build and $300 monthly. Timeline changed from six weeks to eight weeks after adding Slack approvals. Deferred or out-of-scope items included GoHighLevel work for this engagement (deferred to a separate Q3 conversation). Key decision: Add a Slack approval workflow to remove the post-ingestion approval bottleneck. Immediate next step: Send the proposal to Sarah Chen and Patricia Walsh.
scopeIn: Exception handling and human review queue
Slack approval workflow for invoice approvals
scopeOut: GoHighLevel work for this engagement (deferred to a separate Q3 conversation)
commercialNotes: Slack approval workflow increment: $3,000 build and $300 monthly
timelineNotes: Timeline changed from six weeks to eight weeks after adding Slack approvals
Target deployment before the June 30 fiscal year close
Planned phased delivery: Phase 1 ingestion, Phase 2 approvals, Phase 3 production cutover
decisionsAndSignals: Add a Slack approval workflow to remove the post-ingestion approval bottleneck.
Keep GoHighLevel out of the current engagement and defer it to a separate Q3 track.
Proceed with N8N as the orchestration layer as long as the client retains ownership and avoids lock-in.
Structure delivery in three phases so the client sees ingestion working in the first three weeks.
Patricia Walsh reacted positively to the Slack approval workflow addition.
nextSteps: Send the proposal to Sarah Chen and Patricia Walsh.
Structure the proposal as a phased delivery plan with working ingestion in the first three weeks.

decisions.summary: Add a Slack approval workflow to remove the post-ingestion approval bottleneck.
decisions.rationale: Manual approvals remained the gating step even if invoice ingestion was automated.

decisions.summary: Keep GoHighLevel out of the current engagement and defer it to a separate Q3 track.
Proceed with N8N as the orchestration layer as long as the client retains ownership and avoids lock-in.
Structure delivery in three phases so the client sees ingestion working in the first three weeks.
decisions.rationale: Patricia Walsh asked for an early working milestone to reduce risk.

actionItems.summary: Send the proposal to Sarah Chen and Patricia Walsh.
Structure the proposal as a phased delivery plan with working ingestion in the first three weeks.

futureWorkItems.summary: Consider GoHighLevel work later under a separate budget and Q3 conversation.

approvalSignals.summary: Patricia Walsh reacted positively to the Slack approval workflow addition.

risks.summary: Extraction errors could send incorrect invoice data into QuickBooks.
Manual approvals can remain a bottleneck even if invoice ingestion is automated.

scopeChanges.summary: GoHighLevel was deferred out of the AP automation engagement and moved to a later Q3 track.
Slack approvals were added after Bryan Mendez identified manual approvals as the remaining bottleneck.

commercialSnapshots.notes: Slack approval workflow increases the build and monthly support price.

timelineMilestones.summary: The workflow needs to be live before fiscal year close
Kick off by April 1 to maintain a comfortable buffer before fiscal close
